perturbation.original_text: 3. Return of Confidential Information 3.1 EFCA shall: 3.1.1 immediately upon re ceipt of a written reques t from the disclos ing part, return to the disc losing part y all doc uments and materials (and all copies thereof) containing t he disclos ing party’s Confidential Information and certify in writing to the dis closing par ty that it has complied with the requirements of this sub-clause; and 3.1.2 notwithstanding the completi on of any purpose for which any Confidential Informati on has been disclosed or the return of documents and materials as aforesaid, continue to the bound by t he undertakings set out i n clauses 2, 4 and 5.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Return of Confidential Information 3.1 EFCA shall: 3.1.1 immediately upon re ceipt of a written reques t from the disclos ing part, return to the disc losing part y all doc uments and materials (and all copies thereof) containing t he disclos ing party’s Confidential Information and certify in writing to the dis closing par ty that it has complied with the requirements of this sub-clause; and 3.1.2 notwithstanding the completi on of any purpose for which any Confidential Informati on has been disclosed or the return of documents and materials as aforesaid, continue to be bound by t he undertakings set out i n clauses 4 and 5.
perturbation.explanation: By removing clause 2 from sub-clause 3.1.2, the clause is contradicting itself because it originally stated that EFCA would still be bound by clause 2, after returning the documents, but after the changed text is no longer valid. EFCA no longer has to be bound by the undertakings set out in clause 2. This creates uncertainty in section 3 of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1.2 notwithstanding the completi on of any purpose for which any Confidential Informati on has been disclosed or the return of documents and materials as aforesaid, continue to be bound by t he undertakings set out i n clauses 4 and 5.
perturbation.justification: The original text in clause 3.1.2 stated that EFCA would still be bound by clauses 2, 4 and 5 after returning the confidential information. The changed text removes clause 2 from this list, so EFCA is no longer bound by it. This creates a direct conflict with the initial obligation, making it a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the definition of Structural Flaws, and therefore it is a 'YES'.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Limitations and Warrant y 4.1 EFCA shall: 4.1.1 not divulge the di sclosing party’s Confidenti al Information, in whole or in part, to any third party; and 4.1.2 make no c ommercial use of t he same or any par t thereof without the prior wr itten consent of the di sclosing party. No twithstanding the 2 foregoing, EFCA shall be entitled to make any disc losure required by law of t he disc losing party’s Confide ntial Information provide d that it gives the disc losing party not less t han two business days’ notice of such disclosure. 4.2 The disc losing party reserves all ri ghts in its Confidential Information and no rights or obligations other t han those expressly recited herein are granted or to be implied from this Agreement. In particular, no licenc e is hereby granted directly or ind irectly under any patent, invention, discovery, copyright or ot her industrial property right now or in the future held, made, obtained or licen sable by the other party. Nothing herein contained shall be deemed to impose on either party any obligation to giv e the other party the opportunity of giving a quotation or making a tender to the ot her party or to enter into any contract for services or for supply of goods and/or materials with the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Limitations and Warrant y 4.1 EFCA shall: 4.1.1 not divulge the di sclosing party’s Confidenti al Information, in whole or in part, to any third party, unless required by a subpoena; and 4.1.2 make no c ommercial use of t he same or any par t thereof without the prior wr itten consent of the di sclosing party. No twithstanding the 2 foregoing, EFCA shall be entitled to make any disc losure required by law of t he disc losing party’s Confide ntial Information provide d that it gives the disc losing party not less t han two business days’ notice of such disclosure. 4.2 The disc losing party reserves all ri ghts in its Confidential Information and no rights or obligations other t han those expressly recited herein are granted or to be implied from this Agreement. In particular, no licenc e is hereby granted directly or ind irectly under any patent, invention, discovery, copyright or ot her industrial property right now or in the future held, made, obtained or licen sable by the other party. Nothing herein contained shall be deemed to impose on either party any obligation to giv e the other party the opportunity of giving a quotation or making a tender to the ot her party or to enter into any contract for services or for supply of goods and/or materials with the other party.
perturbation.explanation: The change in 4.1.1 adds 'unless required by a subpoena' to the confidentiality restriction. This seemingly clarifies an exception. But the contradictory statement is when 4.1.1 is read in conjunction with the paragraph after it, which requires EFCA to notify the disclosing party of a required disclosure, and giving them at least two business days of notice. But there's no timeframe on how long it will take to subpoena someone. A subpoena is a legal mandate to make a disclosure. Subpoenas are typically urgent and don't accommodate two-day notices. Therefore the two clauses are contradicting themselves in the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 EFCA shall: 4.1.1 not divulge the di sclosing party’s Confidenti al Information, in whole or in part, to any third party, unless required by a subpoena; and 4.1.2 make no c ommercial use of t he same or any par t thereof without the prior wr itten consent of the di sclosing party. No twithstanding the 2 foregoing, EFCA shall be entitled to make any disc losure required by law of t he disc losing party’s Confide ntial Information provide d that it gives the disc losing party not less t han two business days’ notice of such disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds "unless required by a subpoena" to the confidentiality restriction in 4.1.1. The contradicted text states that EFCA must give the disclosing party two business days' notice of any required disclosure. A subpoena is a legal mandate that usually requires immediate action and does not allow for a two-day notice period. Therefore, the added subpoena exception directly contradicts the notice requirement, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The changed text makes compliance with both sections impossible in certain situations.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Confidentiality EFCA agr ees to keep the existenc e and nature of this Agreement confidential and not to use the same or the name of the disclos ing party (or of any company in the Group of Companies of which the disclosing party forms part) in any public ity, adv ertisement or other disclosure with regard to this Agreement without the pr ior written consent of the disclos ing party.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Confidentiality EFCA agr ees to keep the existenc e and nature of this Agreement confidential and not to use the same or the name of the disclos ing party (or of any company in the Group of Companies of which the disclosing party forms part) in any public ity, adv ertisement or other disclosure with regard to this Agreement; however, EFCA is free to disclose the details of the agreement to its legal counsel, financial advisors and potential investors without the prior written consent of the disclos ing party.
perturbation.explanation: The original text strictly prohibits any disclosure of the agreement without prior written consent. The added clause allows EFCA to disclose details to specific parties (legal counsel, financial advisors, potential investors) without consent. This directly contradicts the first part of the section, creating uncertainty about the scope of confidentiality required. This internal contradiction occurs within Section 5.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Confidentiality EFCA agr ees to keep the existenc e and nature of this Agreement confidential and not to use the same or the name of the disclos ing party (or of any company in the Group of Companies of which the disclosing party forms part) in any public ity, adv ertisement or other disclosure with regard to this Agreement; however, EFCA is free to disclose the details of the agreement to its legal counsel, financial advisors and potential investors without the prior written consent of the disclos ing party.
perturbation.justification: The original text of Section 5 strictly prohibits any disclosure of the agreement without prior written consent. The changed text adds an exception, allowing EFCA to disclose details to specific parties (legal counsel, financial advisors, potential investors) without consent. This directly contradicts the initial prohibition within the same Section 5, creating a strong in-text contradiction because the same section both prohibits and allows disclosure without consent, thus meeting the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: 9. Entire Agreement, Governing La w and Jurisdiction This Agreement constitutes the entire Agreement and under standing between the parties in respect of C onfidentia l Information and supersedes all previous agreement s, understandings and undertakings in suc h respect. This Agreement cannot be c hanged except by wr itten agreement between t he parties. The inter pretation, construction and effect of this Agreement shall be enforced in accor danc e with the laws of the State of Victoria. This agree ment will b e effective as of the date below, and contin ue for five (5) years.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Entire Agreement, Governing La w and Jurisdiction This Agreement constitutes the entire Agreement and under standing between the parties in respect of C onfidentia l Information and supersedes all previous agreement s, understandings and undertakings in suc h respect. This Agreement can be changed by written agreement between the parties or by EFCA providing 30 days written notice to the disclosing party. The inter pretation, construction and effect of this Agreement shall be enforced in accor danc e with the laws of the State of Victoria. This agree ment will b e effective as of the date below, and contin ue for five (5) years.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states the agreement can only be changed by written agreement between the parties. By adding 'or by EFCA providing 30 days written notice to the disclosing party,' we introduce a way for EFCA to unilaterally change the agreement. This is a direct contradiction to the first sentence regarding changing the agreement, which introduces uncertainty and in-text contradiction within the same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Entire Agreement, Governing La w and Jurisdiction This Agreement constitutes the entire Agreement and under standing between the parties in respect of C onfidentia l Information and supersedes all previous agreement s, understandings and undertakings in suc h respect. This Agreement can be changed by written agreement between the parties or by EFCA providing 30 days written notice to the disclosing party. The inter pretation, construction and effect of this Agreement shall be enforced in accor danc e with the laws of the State of Victoria. This agree ment will b e effective as of the date below, and contin ue for five (5) years.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement can only be changed by written agreement between both parties. The changed text introduces an alternative method for EFCA to unilaterally modify the agreement by providing 30 days' notice. This directly contradicts the original clause, which mandated mutual agreement for any changes. The contradicted text is the same section where the change was introduced. The contradiction arises because the agreement now contains two conflicting statements about how it can be modified, which creates in-text contradiction as defined by Structural Flaws.
